title: Build a Light Sensor
description: Use a photoresistor and Arduino to measure ambient brightness.

dialogue.text: Hey again! Orion here. Let's build a light sensor. Place everything on a wooden or plastic work area, touch grounded metal to dump static, keep hands dry, and unplug the board while wiring so nothing shorts out.
Collect an Arduino Uno, a breadboard, three jumper wires, a photoresistor, a 10 kΩ resistor, a USB cable, and a laptop running the Arduino IDE. Lay parts out separately so metal leads can't touch.

Unplug the USB cable. On the breadboard, tie one leg of the photoresistor to 5 V and the other leg to one side of the 10 kΩ resistor. Join the resistor's free leg to GND, then run a jumper from their junction to A0. Verify the resistor value and ensure no bare wires contact before restoring power.

Plug the USB cable back in and upload a sketch that reads A0 and prints to the serial monitor. Shade and expose the sensor to watch the values shift. Skip lasers and avoid staring into bright lamps during testing.
Nice work! Unplug the board before making changes, then coil the USB cable and store all parts for future builds.
